title: koeleria caucasica subsp. fominii
intro: Koeleria caucasica subsp. fominii is a type of perennial grass that belongs to the Poaceae family, native to the mountainous regions of the Caucasus. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Koeleria caucasica subsp. fominii can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to treat various ailments.
